title: S2 Works Re Arranged
wikitext:
[]: S2 Works Re-Arranged             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Tue Jan 11, 2011 9:23 pm FORWARD: Below, is what I consider to be a sane representation of what is heard within the gargantuan s2 Works; Shiro Sagisu's complete recording sessions from both the original series as well as EOE. Listening to the set as presented proved nearly impossible, as pieces where ordered by their numerical IDs, by Sagisu, before Anno came along and titled them. After watching the original series closely, I have built a chronological listening experience of what was heard within the series, with a few outtakes that I felt could have fit within a few select scenes. To prove the worth of the songwriting and Anno's song placement within the series, you can actually hear the story progress as you move through the arcs (each filling one disc). To sum this up, I feel fairly confident that this is the best listening experience possible using the materials provided. Enjoy. DISC 1: Beginning Arc 01 A-8 Angel's Arrival 02 E-6 Angel Attack 03 C-2 Barefoot in the Park 04 A-16 Theme of NERV 05 A-6 I.Shinji 06 A-1 Rei I 07 E-3 EVA-01 08 E-9 A Step Forward Into Terror [No Intro Fanfare] 09 A-5 Advancement of the Human Instrumentality Project 10 C-7 Tokyo-3 11 B-16 Misato 12 B-17 Asuka Strikes! [Rhythm Only] 13 A-2 Background Music I 14 B-4 Fly Me to the Moon 15 E-5 EVA-00 [Fast Tempo, Rhythm Only] (Harbinger of Tragedy) 16 E-5 EVA-00 [Fast Tempo] (The Beast I) 17 F-2 Next Episode 18 C-4 Waking Up in the Morning 19 B-1 Hedgehog's Dilemma 20 E-1 Decisive Battle [Rhythm Only] (Spending Time in Preparation) 21 E-9 A Step Forward Into Terror 22 B-10 Menace and Surprise [No Guitar] 23 E-7 Marking Time, Waiting for Death 24 E-11 No Menu Listing 25 E-5 EVA-00 [Rhythm Only] 26 C-6 A Crystalline Night Sky 27 E-1 Decisive Battle 28 B-8 Determination and Anger 29 B-20 Rei II 30 B-7 When I Find Peace of Mind Running Time - 1:08:24 DISC 2: Action Arc 01 F-0 Eyecatcher I [No Spoken Lines] 02 B-17 Asuka Strikes! 03 E-4 EVA-02 04 C-3 Relaxed and Heartwarming Day 05 B-22 Fly Me to the Moon [B-Type] 06 B-18 Feeling at Peace 07 E-2 Suspense 08 D-1 Violent Situations With Guns 09 A-7 Angel Attack II 10 C-5 Ritsuko 11 A-3 NERV [Rhythm Only] 12 F-2 Next Episode [Simple Version] 13 B-12 Both of You, Dance Like You Want to Win! 14 C-1 Pleasant Morning 15 B-21 A Moment When Tension Breaks [Fast Tempo] 16 A-16 Theme of NERV [Strings] (Background Music II) 17 E-10 No Menu Listing [Rhythm Only] 18 E-9 A Step Forward Into Terror [Rhythm Only] 19 E-15 Magmadiver 20 E-15 Magmadiver [Fast Tempo] 21 B-14 The Day Tokyo-3 Stood Still 22 A-9 Angel Attack III 23 A-3 NERV 24 B-13 Opression and Impatience 25 B-10 Menace and Surprise 26 E-12 Battle I 27 A-15 She said, "Don't make others suffer for you personal hatred." 28 OP-1 A Cruel Angel's Thesis 29 E-2 Suspense [No Brass] 30 E-8 Pleasure Principle Running Time - 1:01:31 DISC 3: Descent Arc 01 F-1 Eyecatcher II [No Spoken Lines] 02 A-4 Borderline Case [Piano, Lonely Version] 03 A-13 Rei I [A-Type] 04 F-3 Cello Solo Melody [Take 1] 05 C-55 Ritsuko [E-Type] 06 B-4 Fly Me to the Moon [Strings and Piano] ("Those women longed for the touch of others' lips, and thus invited their kisses.") 07 A-4 Borderline Case 08 A-13 Separation Anxiety 09 A-10 Mother Is the First Other [Synth Voice Only] (Splitting of the Breast) 10 E-16 The Beast II [Rhythm Only] 11 B-15 Liberation, Catalysis 12 D-7 Beautiful 13 B-22 Fly Me to the Moon [B-Type] 14 B-19 Friendship 15 B-3 Background Music III 16 E-5 EVA-00 17 A-9 Angel Attack III [Synth Voice Only] 18 A-7 Angel Attack II [Synth Only] 19 E-14 Battle II [Rhythm] 20 B-6 Anxiety 21 E-6 The Beast II [Fast Tempo] (Normal Blood) 22 D-9 Plaintive 23 B-4 Fly Me to the Moon [Guitar and Piano] 24 E-13 Thanatos 25 E-16 The Beast II Running Time - 0:48:33 DISC 4: Bitter End Arc 01 A-4 Borderline Case [With Piano, Mellow Unison] 02 A-11 Depression [Synth Voice Only] 03 B-9 Infantile Dependance, Adult Dependancy 04 B-4 Fly Me to the Moon [Piano] 05 B-2 In the Depths of Human Hearts 06 A-13 Rei I [B-Type] (Crime of Innocence) 07 C-55 Ritsuko [D-Type] (Three of Me, One of Someone Else) 08 A-13 Rei I [C-Type] (Hostility Restrained) 09 B-4 Fly Me to the Moon [Piano, Lonely] (The Sorrow of Losing the Object of One's Dependence) 10 B-5 Requiem, Sorrow 11 D-10 Hastiness 12 A-7 Angel Attack II [Rhythm & Synth] 13 A-4 Borderline Case [Piano, Normal Version] 14 A-4 Borderline Case [Strings Version] 15 A-4 Borderline Case [Lonely Version] 16 A-7 Angel Attack II [Bridge] 17 E-10 No Menu Listing 18 E-14 Battle II 19 E-13 Thanatos [Rhythm Only] 20 A-14 Rei III 21 B-4 Fly Me to the Moon [Strings] 22 A-11 Depression 23 A-12 Introjection 24 A-13 Separation Anxiety [Rhythm Only] 25 A-4 Borderline Case [Synth Voice Only] (Do You Love Me?) 26 B-9 Infantile Dependance, Adult Dependancy [No Rhythm] 27 A-10 Mother Is the First Other 28 B-21 A Moment When Tension Breaks 29 OP-1 A Cruel Angel's Thesis [Strings, Slow Tempo] (The Heady Feeling of Freedom) 30 OP-1 A Cruel Angel's Thesis [D-Type] (Good, or Don't Be) Running Time - 1:04:39 DISC 5: Death 01 Cello Tuning - Cello Fourth Cord - Tuning Cord 02 Suiten fur Violoncello solo Nr.1 G-dur, BWV. 1007 1.Vorspier 03 DVORAK: Original Complete Version 04 Violin Tuning - Violin Second Cord - Tuning Cord 05 Partita III fur Violino solo E-dur, BWV. 1006 3. Gavotte in Rondo 06 Viola Tuning - Viola Third Cord - Tuning Cord 07 D-3 Gentle Substitute 08 Tuning Panorama [Final Tune] 09 E-13 Pessimistic and Tragic Feeling [Fast Tempo With Piano] (Towards Oneself, Tears) 10 Kanon D-dur [Quintet] 11 B-4 Fly Me to the Moon [Guitar & Strings, Lonely] (The Sorrow of Losing the Object of One's Dependence II) 12 A-4 Borderline Case [Violin] (Dependence to Emptiness) 13 A-4 Borderline Case [Different Version] (Fragility, Ego Boundary) 14 Kanon D-dur [Strings Orchestra] 15 OP-2 A Cruel Angel's Thesis [C-Type] Running Time - 0:24:07 DISC 6: The End of Evangelion 01 M-6 [Fast Tempo, Rhythm Only] (Interference of Others) 02 M-6 [Fast Tempo, Modified] (The End of Midsummer) 03 M-4 Escape to the Beginning [Chorus Only] (Emergency Evacuation to Regression) 04 M-7B False Regeneration 05 E-13 Pessimistic and Tragic Feeling [Rhythm Only, Modified] (Substitute Invasion) 06 II Air (Orchestral suite No.3 in D Major, BWV. 1068) 07 M-14 (Unedited) 08 M-3 The Passage of Emptiness 09 M-8 Asuka's Death 10 Thanatos ~ If I Can't Be Yours 11 M-4 Escape to the Beginning 12 M-2 Light Etude 13 M-5 Tragic Choral Music 14 A-4 Borderline Case [Zakazaka Version] (Honeymoon With Anxiety) 15 M-10 Komm, süsser Tod [Single Version] 16 Jesus bleibet meine Freude (Herz und Mund und Tat und Leben BWV.147) [Strings] 17 M-9 Liberation of Souls [Modified] (Expansion of Blockade] 18 M-15 (Unfinished) 19 A-4 Borderline Case [Piano/Omakase Version] (Opening of a Dream) 20 Everything You've Ever Dreamed Running Time - 1:18:30 150 Tracks Total Running Time - 5:45:44[/i]

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Jan 11, 2011 11:57 pm I don't get it. You see, this is why I need mod powers, you guys. This thread would be locked by now. But oh no, we have to wait for Ursus to come by and do it instead.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Jan 12, 2011 12:02 am I don't get it either. Is this listing a re-naming of the songs from S2 Works? According to the EvaWiki's article the original S2 works only had cut names and titles like the ones shown here. I guess it's a fan arrangement, but it would have been nice if there was actual information in the opening post.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Wed Jan 12, 2011 1:07 am Maybe it's supposed to be art? Modern.. art? uhh.

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Wed Jan 12, 2011 1:19 am Not impressed? Well, this was just my own fan mix of how I would like S2 works to play out. I figured it was self explanatory. Thought I would share it with the world. Note, not every cue is included, just most of the ones in the show and a few I thought could fit in there. Take it or leave it. smh

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Jan 12, 2011 3:27 am thedarkorb wrote: Well, this was just my own fan mix of how I would like S2 works to play out. I figured it was self explanatory.

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Wed Jan 12, 2011 4:08 am Forward added. This is for Sagisu aficionados only. EvangelionFan wrote: Also, are the images you included scans from the S2 collection boxart or custom cover art?

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Wed Jan 12, 2011 8:01 am thedarkorb wrote: Not impressed? Well, this was just my own fan mix of how I would like S2 works to play out. I figured it was self explanatory. Thought I would share it with the world. Note, not every cue is included, just most of the ones in the show and a few I thought could fit in there. Take it or leave it. smh

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Fri Jan 14, 2011 11:14 pm Eh, figured it was common terminology around here. This site is called EvaGEEKS you know. You should give it a shot if you have the patience for it. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Fri Jan 14, 2011 11:20 pm -

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Fri Jan 14, 2011 11:53 pm Death does include the beginning of EOE, so it makes sense to include the one track that were featured in that included segment, yes? I'm pretty sure just False Regeneration was included.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sat Jan 15, 2011 1:19 am thedarkorb wrote: Death does include the beginning of EOE, so it makes sense to include the one track that were featured in that included segment, yes? I'm pretty sure just False Regeneration was included.

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Thu Apr 07, 2011 5:17 pm * Edit made to discs 3 & 4. Weaving a Story II has been moved to the start of disc 4, with disc 3 ending on The Beast II (the scene where Eva-01 eats the angel). Descent and Bitter End arcs (the last two) begin on the Weaving a Story episodes anyways.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Thu Apr 07, 2011 6:14 pm Decent arrangement and this has been discussed in the past (I recall Reichu listing the tracks from S2 and also mentioning how jumbled the order was, and something about how she thought she might take a crack at it some time). But why the rhythm only version of Thanatos and Rei III in the descent arc? That version of Thanatos was from the elevator scene in EoE, and Rei III came at the end of EoE. Just curious. The series version of Thanatos would be much better alone there, since it was the only one played in the series (on 3 separate occasions, I think).

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Thu Apr 07, 2011 6:41 pm If you read the intro at the top, I stated that I tried to insert a few alternate takes and ommited tracks into the mix. Reichu did do a mix too, but the site where it was hosted cut off the second half of the listing, so I figured I would do it myself and add in more tracks from the S2 Works. The full form Thanatos plays here right before Shinji has his battle with Zeruel (disc 3, 24). In the show, it does play again (the same version) before Rei's death, though I tracked in the more minimal Rhythm version (disc 4, 20). This version should not be confused with Substitute Invasion from EOE (disc 6, 5), those are two seperate recordings of the same theme. Rei III does not play anywhere in the show or the film, but I felt it sat at a good place after Rei's death in the show (disc 4, 21) as her finale. Again, this was not meant to be a 100% accurate list of what plays in the show, but an alternate way to take in the S2 Works, as a whole, in a sitting.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 07, 2011 7:51 pm toe mash wrote: Decent arrangement and this has been discussed in the past (I recall Reichu listing the tracks from S2 and also mentioning how jumbled the order was, and something about how she thought she might take a crack at it some time).

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Thu Apr 07, 2011 10:11 pm Added on cue numbers. Hopefully it will make putting it together a little easier.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 07, 2011 10:31 pm I oughta put together my MP3s of this sometime and give it a listen, looks cool. (I swear if S2 Works would be re-released I'd totally buy it. And there was a place I used to live buy that had a legit copy but I didn't have enough money at the time to get it... X_x I have so much Eva merchandise anyway...)

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Thu Apr 07, 2011 10:46 pm If you're into Shiro Sagisu or the score in general, it's a good listen. :) I like the extened EOE quite a bit, I think "Everything You've Ever Dreamed" ends it on a (EDIT: MORBIDLY DEPRESSING) note.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 07, 2011 11:18 pm thedarkorb wrote: I think "Everything You've Ever Dreamed" ends it on a high note .

atos was from the elevator scene in EoE, and Rei III came at the end of EoE. Just curious. The series version of Thanatos would be much better alone there, since it was the only one played in the series (on 3 separate occasions, I think).

Reichu did do a mix too, but the site where it was hosted cut off the second half of the listing, so I figured I would do it myself and add in more tracks from the S2 Works. The full form Thanatos plays here right before Shinji has his battle with Zeruel (disc 3, 24). In the show, it does play again (the same version) before Rei's death, though I tracked in the more minimal Rhythm version (disc 4, 20). This version should not be confused with Substitute Invasion from EOE (disc 6, 5), those are two seperate recordings of the same theme. Rei III does not play anywhere in the show or the film, but I felt it sat at a good place after Rei's death in the show (disc 4, 21) as her finale. Again, this was not meant to be a 100% accurate list of what plays in the show, but an alternate way to take in the S2 Works, as a whole, in a sitting.
